meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Scale Your Student Projects (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project scalability. Use this intermediate checklist to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: To overcome scaling bottlenecks, shift from manual coding to AI-assisted architectural design. By integrating Antigravity for rapid UI prototyping and Claude Code for iterative backend logic, you transform from a student coder into a systems architect. This approach automates the repetitive boilerplate, allowing you to focus on high-level feature deployment.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen filled with spaghetti code, your project grinding to a halt the moment you try to add a second feature. The scaling wall is real, and it is the graveyard of thousands of student projects. You have the logic, but your infrastructure is crumbling under the weight of manual maintenance.

But what if you didn't have to build every line yourself? By leveraging AI-driven design, you shift your role from a typist to a conductor. This guide bridges the gap between 'working code' and 'scalable architecture,' using the precision of Antigravity and the raw power of Claude Code to turn your prototype into a professional-grade application.

steps.title: Architectural Blueprinting
steps.description: Define your data flow and component hierarchy before writing logic to prevent technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and generate a scalable folder structure and modular component architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect 'create project directory structure based on clean architecture principles'

steps.title: Interface Prototyping
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity UI components that are decoupled from your backend logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout using Tailwind CSS with clear separation of concerns for data fetching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'generate components/Dashboard.tsx with mock data structure'

steps.title: API Layer Decoupling
steps.description: Abstract your API calls into a service layer to ensure your frontend remains agnostic to backend changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API service layer that handles authentication and CRUD operations with error handling interceptors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --refactor 'move all axios/fetch calls into a dedicated services/api.ts file'

steps.title: State Management Implementation
steps.description: Implement a centralized state store to handle data flow, preventing prop-drilling as your app scales.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a Zod-validated state management store for user session and dashboard data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement Zustand store for global application state'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suites
steps.description: Scale with confidence by having AI generate unit tests for your critical business logic paths.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest test coverage for the core logic in my service layer, focusing on edge case handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --generate 'create unit tests for services/api.ts functions'

pro_tips: Always define your Zod schemas first; let the AI generate the logic based on the schema, not the other way around.
Use Claude Code to perform 'dry runs' on refactors before applying them to your main branch to avoid breaking changes.
Maintain a 'context file' that summarizes your project's current state to help AI tools provide more accurate, project-aware suggestions.
When scaling, prioritize modularity over cleverness; if a component is over 200 lines, ask Antigravity to split it by responsibility.
Use AI to document your code as you write it, ensuring your project remains maintainable for future portfolio reviewers.
content.next_steps: Once your core architecture is stable, begin integrating a real-world database like Supabase or Neon to transition your project from a local prototype to a live, cloud-hosted application.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting scaling issues limit your potential as a developer. Apply these workflows to your next project and watch your development velocity double overnight. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
